attractions.name: Denali National Park & Preserve
attractions.season: Entrance area year-round; bus season May 20-Sep 17, full services Jun-early Sep
attractions.blurb: 6-million-acre park at Parks Hwy Mile 237. Grizzly, caribou, wolf, moose plus North America's highest peak.
attractions.tip: Only ~30% of visitors see the mountain - build in 2+ nights to beat the clouds. Book buses/lodging months out.
attractions.duration_note: 2 nights minimum; 3 if you want a real hike

attractions.name: Denali Park Road
attractions.season: Bus service May 20-Sep 17; closed at Mile 43 for all of 2026
attractions.cost: $33.50 transit bus to East Fork; kids 15 and under free
attractions.blurb: 92-mile gravel road into the park. Pretty Rocks landslide still cuts it at Mile 43 (East Fork) through 2026.
attractions.tip: Buses turn around at Mile 43 - a 4.5hr round trip. Polychrome/Eielson/Wonder Lake service is targeted for 2027.
attractions.duration_note: 4.5hr round trip to East Fork in 2026

attractions.name: Denali Visitor Center
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September; winter services move to the Murie Center
attractions.blurb: Main orientation stop: natural-history exhibits, ranger programs, the 'Heartbeats of Denali' film, trailheads.
attractions.tip: Free courtesy shuttles to the sled dog kennels and bus depot leave from here - park once and ride.

attractions.name: Denali Bus Depot
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Mile 0.5 of the Park Road: where every transit and tour bus loads, plus campground check-in and a snack bar.
attractions.tip: Buses leave on time and won't wait. Arrive 30 min early; there is no cell service past the entrance area.

attractions.name: Savage River
attractions.season: Road to Mile 15 open ~mid-May to mid-Sep; gated at Mile 13 off-season
attractions.blurb: Farthest point private cars can drive. Flat 2-mile river loop through a glacial canyon; Dall sheep on the ridges.
attractions.tip: Free Savage River Shuttle runs from the visitor center if the small lot is full - it fills by 10am in July.

attractions.name: Savage Alpine Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly mid-June through September
attractions.blurb: 4mi point-to-point, ~1,500ft gain between Savage River Campground (Mile 13) and Savage River (Mile 15).
attractions.tip: Best day hike reachable without a park bus. Hike west-to-east and ride the free shuttle back to your car.

attractions.name: Mount Healy Overlook Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly June-September; icy shoulder seasons
attractions.blurb: 2.7mi one-way, ~1,700ft climb from the visitor center to a treeline bench over the Nenana River valley.
attractions.tip: The single best entrance-area hike, no bus needed. Push 30 min past the overlook to the ridge for wider views.

attractions.name: Horseshoe Lake Trail
attractions.season: May-September; boggy in early spring
attractions.blurb: Easy 2mi loop dropping ~200ft to an oxbow lake off the Nenana River. Active beaver lodges and dams.
attractions.tip: Most popular short hike in the park - go at 7am or after dinner for beaver activity and an empty trail.

attractions.name: Denali Park Sled Dog Kennels
attractions.season: Demos daily late May-early September; kennels open to walk-ins year-round
attractions.blurb: Only working sled-dog kennel in the US National Park System - rangers still patrol Denali by dog team in winter.
attractions.tip: Demos run ~3x daily; take the free shuttle from the visitor center 40 min early and go meet the dogs first.

attractions.name: Eielson Visitor Center
attractions.season: CLOSED for 2026 - road severed at Mile 43; reopening targeted with full bus service in 2027
attractions.blurb: LEED-platinum center 33 miles from Denali itself - the classic head-on view of the mountain. Shut since 2021.
attractions.tip: Do not plan a 2026 trip around Eielson. If it reopens in 2027 the Eielson bus is an 8-hour round trip.

attractions.name: Wonder Lake
attractions.season: Campground and bus service closed for 2026; historically late Jun-mid Sep
attractions.blurb: The Ansel Adams view: 20,310ft Denali reflected in a 2.5-mile tundra lake. Currently unreachable by road.
attractions.tip: Mosquitoes here are legendary - head net, not repellent. Closed in 2026; watch NPS for 2027 reopening.

attractions.name: Kantishna
attractions.aka: Mile 92, end of the Park Road
attractions.season: Lodges early June-mid September; fly-in only while the road is cut
attractions.blurb: Old gold camp at the road's end, now 4 wilderness lodges deep inside the park with guided hiking and rafting.
attractions.tip: With Mile 43 closed, lodges run fly-in packages from Denali Park Airport instead of the 6-hour bus.

attractions.name: Denali Flightseeing & Glacier Landings
attractions.aka: Denali Air, Fly Denali, Kantishna Air Taxi
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September; weather-dependent daily
attractions.blurb: 1-1.5hr flights from Denali Park Airport (Mile 229) and Healy River into the Alaska Range and Ruth Amphitheater.
attractions.tip: With the Park Road cut at Mile 43, this is now the surest way to actually see the mountain. Book a morning slot.

attractions.name: Nenana River Canyon Rafting
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Class III-IV glacial whitewater right outside the park entrance; mellower scenic float option upstream.
attractions.tip: Water is ~38F - take the drysuit upgrade, not the splash jacket. Kids usually need the scenic float instead.

attractions.name: Denali Park Alaska Railroad Depot
attractions.season: Denali Star runs daily mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Where the Denali Star drops you inside the park entrance - no rental car needed for a Denali-only trip.
attractions.tip: Northbound train arrives ~4pm, southbound ~12:30pm. Goldstar dome cars sell out first; book by February.

attractions.name: Husky Homestead
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September, 3 tours daily
attractions.blurb: Working kennel of 4-time Iditarod champion Jeff King: puppy handling, gear talk, ATV sled-team demo.
attractions.tip: Tour includes shuttle pickup from Denali-area hotels. Puppies are usually born in spring - best litters in June.

attractions.name: Healy
attractions.season: Year-round; the only Denali-area town that stays open in winter
attractions.blurb: Coal-mining town 11 miles north of the park entrance - real grocery, gas, clinic and cheaper year-round lodging.
attractions.tip: Base here instead of the canyon strip: rooms run $80-150 less and it is the only winter/aurora option near Denali.

attractions.name: 49th State Brewing Company
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: Big brewpub in Healy with an Alaska Range beer garden and the replica Bus 142 used in the film 'Into the Wild'.
attractions.tip: The bus in the yard is the movie prop, not the real one - Bus 142 is in Fairbanks. No reservations; expect a wait at 7pm.

attractions.name: Cantwell
attractions.season: Year-round; services thin outside summer
attractions.blurb: Parks Hwy Mile 210 junction village, western gateway to the Denali Highway. Fuel, a lodge and a small store.
attractions.tip: Last reliable gas before 135 miles of gravel east to Paxson. Top off here, both directions.

attractions.name: Denali Highway
attractions.season: Open ~mid-May to Oct 1; unmaintained and closed to cars in winter
attractions.blurb: 135 miles, 112 of them gravel, along the south flank of the Alaska Range. Glaciers, eskers, caribou, zero towns.
attractions.tip: Most rental contracts forbid it - check first. No fuel or cell service between Cantwell and Paxson; carry a spare.

attractions.name: Maclaren Summit
attractions.season: Accessible while the Denali Highway is open, roughly mid-May to Oct 1
attractions.blurb: 4,086ft pass, second-highest road summit in Alaska, looking straight at the Maclaren Glacier and Mt Hayes.
attractions.tip: Best light is evening. Walk the tundra ridge north of the pullout 15 min for the full glacier panorama.

attractions.name: Nenana
attractions.season: Year-round; Ice Classic tripod goes out in late February, breakup usually late April-early May
attractions.blurb: Tanana River town halfway Fairbanks-Denali, home of the Nenana Ice Classic breakup lottery since 1917.
attractions.tip: Stop at the 1923 railroad depot museum and the Alfred Starr Cultural Center - both free, both 5 min off the highway.

attractions.name: Fairbanks
attractions.season: Year-round; midnight sun late May-July, aurora late Aug-mid Apr
attractions.blurb: Alaska's second city at 64.8N: aurora capital, gateway to the Dalton and Steese, base for Chena Hot Springs.
attractions.tip: Aurora season restarts around Aug 21 when nights get dark again - late Aug pairs green skies with green tundra.

attractions.name: University of Alaska Museum of the North
attractions.season: Year-round; 9-7 summer, 9-5 winter
attractions.blurb: Best museum in Interior Alaska: Blue Babe the 36,000-year-old steppe bison, Native art, and a striking Kahn building.
attractions.tip: Bus 142 from 'Into the Wild' is here but NOT on display in 2026 - only related artifacts, pending exhibit funding.

attractions.name: Pioneer Park
attractions.season: Buildings and shops open late May-early September; grounds open year-round
attractions.blurb: 44-acre pioneer theme park: relocated gold-rush cabins, the sternwheeler Nenana, an aviation and a rail museum.
attractions.tip: Free to walk in, and the Alaska Salmon Bake here is the easy dinner. Buildings shut by mid-September.

attractions.name: Riverboat Discovery
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September, 2 sailings daily
attractions.blurb: 3.5hr sternwheeler on the Chena and Tanana with a bush-plane demo, Susan Butcher's kennel, and an Athabascan village stop.
attractions.tip: Book the 8:45am sailing - afternoon runs are the cruise-bus crowd. The Chena Indian Village walk is the real content.

attractions.name: Large Animal Research Station
attractions.season: Guided tours June-August; viewing deck open year-round
attractions.blurb: UAF research herd of muskox, caribou and reindeer on Yankovich Rd - guaranteed close-up muskox, no luck required.
attractions.tip: Take the guided tour, not the free fence view. Qiviut (muskox underwool) is combed out in spring - May calves are a bonus.

attractions.name: Running Reindeer Ranch
attractions.season: Year-round by reservation; snowshoe walks in winter
attractions.blurb: Family ranch in the boreal forest where you walk loose in the woods with a small herd of habituated reindeer.
attractions.tip: Reservation only, small groups, sells out weeks ahead in summer. Bring bug spray - it is real forest.

attractions.name: Morris Thompson Cultural & Visitors Center
attractions.season: Year-round; extended summer hours, free daily programs Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Free downtown center on the Chena: Athabascan culture exhibits, a full-size fish camp, plus the state public-lands desk.
attractions.tip: Free Athabascan dance, craft and storytelling programs run most summer afternoons - check the daily board on arrival.

attractions.name: Gold Dredge 8
attractions.season: Mid-May to mid-September
attractions.blurb: National Historic Landmark bucket-line dredge on Goldstream Creek; narrow-gauge train ride and guaranteed gold panning.
attractions.tip: El Dorado Gold Mine has closed - its tour merged into this one. Everyone finds gold; bring cash to have it weighed.

attractions.name: Trans-Alaska Pipeline Viewpoint
attractions.aka: Alyeska Pipeline Viewing Point, Steese Hwy Mile 8.4
attractions.season: Year-round; staffed info trailer late May-early September
attractions.blurb: Best roadside look at the 800-mile pipeline: elevated zigzag section, heat-pipe supports, and a cutaway 'pig' on display.
attractions.tip: Free, always open, 15 min from downtown. Pair it with Gold Dredge 8 a mile up the road - same trip.

attractions.name: Fountainhead Antique Auto Museum
attractions.season: Year-round; daily in summer, limited winter days
attractions.blurb: Roughly 90 pre-WWII cars, most still drivable, paired with period fashion and Alaska's own automotive history.
attractions.tip: Consistently the highest-rated indoor stop in Fairbanks - the obvious rainy-day or aurora-nap-day plan.

attractions.name: World Ice Art Championships
attractions.aka: Ice Alaska, George Horner Ice Park
attractions.season: Late February through late March
attractions.blurb: Month-long international ice-carving competition using famously clear Fairbanks pond ice, plus a kids' ice playground.
attractions.tip: Go after dark when the sculptures are lit - and March pairs it with peak aurora season and tolerable temperatures.

attractions.name: World Eskimo-Indian Olympics
attractions.season: Four days in mid-to-late July
attractions.blurb: Since 1961: ear pull, blanket toss, knuckle hop and seal-skinning contests drawing Native athletes from across Alaska.
attractions.tip: Buy a day pass and stay for the evening session - that is when the finals and the blanket toss happen.

attractions.name: Cleary Summit
attractions.season: Aurora late August-mid April; ski hill Dec-Mar
attractions.blurb: 2,200ft ridge 20 miles north of Fairbanks with a north-facing horizon above the valley fog - the classic aurora pullout.
attractions.tip: Above the inversion, so it can be 20F warmer than town. Aurora peaks 11pm-3am; the Skiland lodge opens for tours.

attractions.name: Fairbanks International Airport
attractions.season: Year-round; most seasonal service Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Interior Alaska's air gateway: jets from Anchorage and Seattle, plus the bush hub for Coldfoot, Bettles and the Arctic.
attractions.tip: Flying open-jaw into Anchorage and out of Fairbanks avoids backtracking the 360-mile Parks Highway.

attractions.name: Chena Hot Springs Resort
attractions.season: Open 365 days; pool house 7am-11:45pm
attractions.blurb: 106F outdoor rock lake at the end of a 56-mile paved road, 60 miles from Fairbanks. Alaska's marquee aurora soak.
attractions.tip: Rock lake is 18+; kids get the indoor pool. Aurora season is late Aug-mid Apr - sit in the water facing north after 11pm.

attractions.name: Aurora Ice Museum
attractions.season: Year-round, ~4 guided tours daily
attractions.blurb: Year-round ice structure at Chena Hot Springs kept at 25F by geothermal-powered chillers - carved bar, beds, chandeliers.
attractions.tip: Tour times are fixed and sell out; book when you buy your hot springs pass. Parkas provided, but wear real shoes.

attractions.name: Chena River State Recreation Area
attractions.season: Campgrounds late May-mid September; road plowed year-round
attractions.blurb: 254,000 acres along Chena Hot Springs Rd: catch-and-release Arctic grayling, canoe floats, 3 campgrounds, granite tors.
attractions.tip: Chena grayling is fly-only catch-and-release. Rosehip and Tors campgrounds are first-come and fill on summer weekends.

attractions.name: Angel Rocks Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly late May-September
attractions.blurb: 3.5mi loop, ~900ft gain, to house-sized granite outcrops above the Chena River - the best short hike near Fairbanks.
attractions.tip: Or hike the 8.3mi one-way traverse to Chena Hot Springs and soak at the end - stash a car at the resort.

attractions.name: Granite Tors Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly June-September; boggy in early summer
attractions.blurb: 15mi loop, ~2,700ft gain, onto alpine tundra studded with granite spires up to 100ft tall - Alaska's tor country.
attractions.tip: Long day, not a stroll. Boardwalk over the first bog then relentless climb; there is a free public shelter at the top.

attractions.name: Santa Claus House
attractions.season: Year-round; reindeer on site, peak hours in summer and December
attractions.blurb: Christmas-year-round gift shop in North Pole with a 42ft Santa, live reindeer, and streets named Santa Claus Lane.
attractions.tip: 15 min south of Fairbanks off the Richardson Hwy. Mail a letter here for a genuine North Pole, AK 99705 postmark.

attractions.name: Delta Junction Visitor Center
attractions.aka: End of the Alaska Highway, Milepost 1422
attractions.season: Visitor center mid-May to mid-September; monument accessible year-round
attractions.blurb: Official end of the Alaska Highway: the Milepost 1422 monument, a mosquito statue, and Alaska Pipeline exhibits.
attractions.tip: Buy the End of the Highway certificate if you drove the Alcan. Delta's free-ranging bison herd is often visible nearby.

attractions.name: Rika's Roadhouse & Big Delta State Historical Park
attractions.season: Grounds year-round; buildings and cafe late May-early September
attractions.blurb: Restored 1910 roadhouse on the Valdez-Fairbanks trail at the Tanana River, with gardens and a WWII signal-corps museum.
attractions.tip: Walk to the riverbank for the pipeline's 1,200ft suspension bridge over the Tanana - best free pipeline photo in Alaska.
